author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
vācya, vācaka⋅ei dui svarūpa tomāra
vācya⋅tava śrī⋅vigraha cidānandākāra

vācaka⋅svarūpa⋅tava śrī⋅kṛṣṇādi nāma
varṇarūpī sarvajīva⋅ānanda⋅viśrāma

ei dui svarūpe tava ananta prakāśa
dayā kari’ deya jīve tomāra vilāsa

kintu jāniyāchi nātha, vācaka⋅svarūpa
vācyāpekṣā dayāmaya⋅ei aparūpa

nāma⋅nāmī bheda nāi⋅vedera vacana
tabu nāma⋅nāmī ha’te adhika karuṇa

kṛṣṇa⋅aparādhe jadi nāme śraddhā kari’
prāṇa bhari’ ḍāke nāma⋅rāma, kṛṣṇa, hari

aparādha dūre jāya, ānanda⋅sāgare
bhāse sei anāyāse rasera pāthāre

vigrahasvarūpa vācye aparādha kari’
śuddhanāmāśraye sei aparādhe tari

bhakativinoda māge śrī⋅rūpa⋅caraṇe
vācaka⋅svarūpa nāme rati anukṣaṇe
en_translation:
Vācya and Vācaka—these two are Your forms. Vācya is Your divine form, the embodiment of eternal knowledge and bliss.

Vācaka is Your holy name, which in the form of syllables is the resting place of all souls' bliss.

In these two forms, Your manifestation is endless; out of mercy, You grant Your divine pastimes to the living entities.

But I have known, O Lord, that Your name is even more merciful than Your form—this is wonderful.

The Vedas declare that there is no difference between the Name and the Named. Still, the Name is more compassionate.

If, even after committing offenses against Kṛṣṇa, one has faith in the Name and calls out names like Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, or Hari with a full heart,

then the offenses vanish, and that person floats effortlessly in the ocean of bliss, in the vast expanse of divine mellows.

Though one may offend the Deity form, one can cross over those offenses by taking shelter of the pure Name.

Bhaktivinoda begs at the lotus feet of Śrī Rūpa for constant attachment to the Holy Name.
